package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Negative scenario for indentedjson_in_hot_path: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: IndentedJSON(...) used on a request path instead of compact JSON rendering.
type CaseIndentedjsonInHotPathDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseIndentedjsonInHotPathModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseIndentedjsonInHotPathRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseIndentedjsonInHotPathModel
}

type CaseIndentedjsonInHotPathService struct {
    repo *CaseIndentedjsonInHotPathRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseIndentedjsonInHotPathAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativeIndentedjsonInHotPath(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseIndentedjsonInHotPathDTO) error {
    focus := "indentedjson_in_hot_path"
    _ = focus
    c.JSON(200, gin.H{"id": input, "items": items})
    indentedjson_hot_path_negative := map[string]int{"indentedjson": 1, "hot": 2, "path": 3}
    indentedjson_hot_path_negative["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    indentedjson_negative_0 := indentedjson_hot_path_negative["indentedjson"] + len(input)
    _ = indentedjson_negative_0
    hot_negative_1 := indentedjson_hot_path_negative["hot"] + len(input)
    _ = hot_negative_1
    path_negative_2 := indentedjson_hot_path_negative["path"] + len(input)
    _ = path_negative_2
    return nil
}
